title: HFrEF with LVEF and renal cutoffs
message: Find eligible patients for a heart failure trial: LVEF at or below 35%, serum creatinine below 2.0 mg/dL, age 18–80, on guideline-directed medical therapy for HFrEF.

title: Reduced EF cardiomyopathy study
message: Match patients for a new study in reduced ejection fraction cardiomyopathy. Inclusion: HFrEF, EF ≤35% on recent echo, adults 18–80. Exclusion: creatinine ≥2.0 mg/dL or dialysis.

title: GDMT-optimized systolic HF cohort
message: Who in our database might qualify for a GDMT optimization trial? Need documented HFrEF, LVEF under 35%, stable on ACE inhibitor or ARNI plus beta-blocker, and creatinine under 2.0 mg/dL.

title: Ischemic cardiomyopathy enrollment
message: Find patients for an ischemic cardiomyopathy trial: ischemic heart disease with HFrEF, LVEF ≤30%, age 40–75, no severe renal impairment (creatinine < 2.5 mg/dL).

title: Heart failure + elevated creatinine screen
message: Screen the EHR for a heart failure study accepting patients with mildly elevated creatinine: HFrEF with LVEF ≤35%, creatinine between 1.5 and 2.0 mg/dL, age 21–80.

title: Post-admission HFrEF survivors
message: Identify candidates for a post-hospitalization heart failure trial: hospitalized for acute decompensated HFrEF, discharged on GDMT, LVEF at or below 35%, age 18–85.

title: Low EF with MRA therapy
message: Find patients for a mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist trial: HFrEF, LVEF ≤35%, already on spironolactone or eplerenone, serum potassium below 5.0 mEq/L, creatinine < 2.0 mg/dL.

title: Broad HF phenotype search
message: Run a trial match for adults with chronic systolic heart failure, depressed left ventricular ejection fraction (≤35%), and acceptable renal function. Return the best candidates for chart review.

title: Structured protocol paste
message:
Find patients for Trial PROTO-HF-042.
Inclusion: age 18–80; HFrEF; LVEF ≤35% within 12 months; creatinine < 2.0 mg/dL; on beta-blocker and RAAS inhibitor.
Exclusion: severe CKD, cardiogenic shock, cardiac transplant.

title: Natural-language study ask
message: I'm opening enrollment for a heart failure with reduced ejection fraction study. Who looks eligible if we need EF at or under thirty-five percent, creatinine under two, and patients already on standard GDMT?
